title: Memory Types: ROM and RAM Technologies
summary: Covers ROM (PROM, EPROM, EEPROM) and RAM (SRAM, DRAM) technologies with their properties, and the basic structure of a memory circuit.
content_points: ROM: non-volatile; read-only; PROM (one-time programmable), EPROM (UV erasable), EEPROM (electrically erasable).
RAM: volatile; read/write; SRAM (fast, expensive), DRAM (slower, denser, cheaper).
Memory structure: 1D array of locations; each location has a unique address; decoding block selects by address.
Address space limited by address bus width: 32-bit bus → max 4 GB.
learning_objectives: Distinguish ROM and RAM, and their sub-technologies
Calculate memory size from address bit width
Explain full vs. partial address decoding
